title: Luffy Lands in Wano and Meets Tama
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11 as Luffy lands in Wano, meets Tama, and immediately collides with the poverty and cruelty of Kaido’s rule.
scene: Luffy washes up in Wano, meets Tama, and discovers a country suffering under Kaido’s rule.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I've heard of her!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: So that's the sea- crossing tongue?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Till now, I've done my work eating only at well- known luxury restaurants.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I thought eating at the infamous float- ing restaurant baratie would be a nice change of pace.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I suppose I got what I asked for, in the worst possible way.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Look, I couldn't care less about all that. Finish your food. That's my only rule.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I'll give you one more dish.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If the next dish I eat isn't worthy of my tongue,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll write that anyone that willingly supports the baratie is a brainless pig.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: I'll cook it!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no way we're letting you do this, idiot! Leave it to me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll floor her with one of my secret dishes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Wanna Go?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: WAAASH! The cooks have started fighting with each other!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: KRAAAASH! You idiots! Stop bothering the other guests!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I'll be the one to serve her a dish! Sit down, ya bloody octopuses!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: TSK... Why now, when the owner's out?! sigh.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I feel bad for you, boy. Why waste your future at a hopeless place like this?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll introduce you to a place that'd be a far better fit for you, if you'd like.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I owe a thing or two to the old man that runs this place.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: I can't just up and leave.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Really? Boring.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Business is about money and fame. That's it. Only second- rate fodder let their emotions rule them.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Well, you probably already knew that.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: After all, people only come to this place for the curiosity!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: True gourmet excites the imagination of all who look upon it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Only truly sump- Tuous dishes, crafted with utmost skill, are worth anything in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: BARATIE HAS NO SUCH WORTH. IT'S JUST A THIRD-Class, COOKIE-CUTTER FEEDERY FOR UNAMBITIOUS RIFFRAF!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can't even bring myself to be indignant. I just pity you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I suppose that aged own- er looks upon this place like some sort of treasure.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why else would he be proud of such a di- Lapidation wreck?!

summary: This section advances the Wano Country Arrival storyline with 32 dialogue beats. Luffy’s first steps in Wano establish Tama’s kindness, the country’s hunger, and the brutality of Kaido’s forces.
